I'll review the codebase systematically. Let me read all the key files first.

blocks.name: StructuredOutput

blocks.text: {"critical_issues":[{"severity":"CRITICAL","file":"src/tools/core-tools.ts:62","issue":"Type/API mismatch on state.skillManager.getSkills(): core-tools.ts awaits it and treats result as {ok, data} but skill-manager.ts:190 returns Promise<Skill[]> directly (plain array). result.ok and result.data will be undefined.","fix":"Remove await: const skills = state.skillManager.getSkills(); — use the returned Skill[] directly without Result wrapper."},{"severity":"CRITICAL","file":"src/tools/core-tools.ts:77-79","issue":"state.skillManager.getSkill() called without await — returns Promise but immediately accessed with .ok and .data. Also calls readContent() which doesn't exist on real SkillManager (only readSkillContent()).","fix":"Await the Promise and use readSkillContent(skillResult.data) instead of readContent()."},{"severity":"CRITICAL","file":"src/tools/core-tools.ts:79","issue":"state.skillManager.readContent() does not exist on the real SkillManager class — only readSkillContent() exists (skill-manager.ts:238). This will throw at runtime.","fix":"Replace readContent() with readSkillContent(skillResult.data) and await the promise."},{"severity":"CRITICAL","file":"src/honcho-client.ts:448-466","issue":"learnFromUser() calls addMessages() but addMessages() applies the daily write safety gate to messages[0].content. When learnFromUser() passes a trace object (serialized JSON ~500+ chars), the safety gate's _lastWriteHash won't match the previous entry's hash, so writes won't be deduplicated correctly — but worse, the 1000/day limit hits after 1000 learnFromUser calls, permanently blocking all writes including legitimate message logging.","fix":"learnFromUser should use its own counter or bypass the safety gate since it stores structured trace data, not user messages."},{"severity":"HIGH","file":"src/index.ts:207-212","issue":"state.skillManager.discover() returns Promise<Skill[]> but is treated as {ok, data} Result type. Same problem as core-tools.ts line 62.","fix":"state.skillManager.discover() returns Skill[] directly. Use: const result = await state.skillManager.discover(); if (result.length > 0) ..."}],"improvements":[{"file":"src/tools/core-tools.ts","suggestion":"Inconsistent API convention: state.honcho methods (search, ask) return {ok, data} while state.router methods return {success, data}. Document which conventions apply and ensure consistency. Consider unifying with a shared Result type or clearly separating the two in JSDoc."},{"file":"src/tools/skill-tools.ts","suggestion":"zcrystal_indexer_rebuild always returns errResult('not yet implemented'). Stub tools returning permanent errors are misleading. Either implement them, mark them experimental, or remove them."},{"file":"src/tools/proactive-tools.ts:281-287","suggestion":"zcrystal_layers_exchange always returns a placeholder string ('Layer exchange is automatic...'). This tool provides no real functionality and will confuse agents that call it. Remove or implement properly."},{"file":"src/tools/proactive-tools.ts:291-299","suggestion":"zcrystal_predict returns hardcoded confidence: 0.6 with fabricated reasons. It calls reviewEngine.getUpgradeSuggestions() but doesn't actually predict user needs. This is misleading. Remove or implement with real prediction logic."},{"file":"src/tools/system-tools.ts:82-96","suggestion":"zcrystal_hook_register always returns errResult telling users the tool requires a handler function — but it's a tool registration function, not a hook. The logic is inverted. zcrystal_hook_list returns hardcoded empty counts. Both are non-functional stubs."},{"file":"src/tools/system-tools.ts:110-118","suggestion":"zcrystal_coordinator_queue and zcrystal_coordinator_status return placeholder strings with no actual status retrieval. These should query actual state from evolutionCoordinator."},{"file":"src/honcho-client.ts","suggestion":"honcho-trace.ts duplicates addMessages/getTraces from honcho-client.ts. These two stores could diverge. Consolidate to a single source of truth."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts:277-290","suggestion":"performRollback() is called fire-and-forget from multiple places without awaiting. If rollback fails, no error is propagated to the caller. This silent failure could lead to persistent degraded skill state."},{"file":"src/index.ts:70-100","suggestion":"fts5Search() and fts5Stats() duplicate logic from fts5-bridge.ts. index.ts has its own HTTP fetch implementations while fts5-bridge.ts has near-identical ones. Consolidate to use fts5-bridge.ts exclusively."},{"file":"src/config.ts","suggestion":"getConfig() uses dynamic key lookup with any-typed value. This is a type-safety hole. Use typed getters or a proper config accessor class."},{"file":"src/onboarding-wizard.ts","suggestion":"Unused module — imported nowhere and only runs via CLI direct invocation (import.meta.url check). If not part of an active setup flow, remove it to reduce dead code."},{"file":"src/user-analyzer.ts","suggestion":"Entire class is unused — no imports anywhere. generateSummary() contains zh-TW localized text (mixed-language codebase). Dead code that should be removed or moved to a separate i18n-aware package."},{"file":"src/index.ts:143-169","suggestion":"Two setInterval background tasks run indefinitely from plugin registration with no cleanup on plugin unregister/deregister. If the plugin is ever unloaded, these intervals will continue running, causing resource leaks."}],"working_well":[{"file":"src/honcho-client.ts","what":"Robust error handling with try/catch throughout; graceful failures return empty arrays/null rather than throwing. Safety gates (_checkAndRecordWrite) are well-placed to prevent runaway writes."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","what":"Two-phase commit (prepareEvolution/commitEvolution) with recovery pointers following the 13-harness-patterns documentation. Closed-loop verification with rollback is architecturally sound. Mutation rules (GEPA-inspired) are extensible."},{"file":"src/skill-manager.ts","what":"Clean recursive skill discovery with depth limit (prevents infinite recursion). Frontmatter parsing with multiple fallback strategies. slugify() is correctly implemented."},{"file":"src/tools/task-tools.ts","what":"Consistent use of result.success convention from state.router. Clean separation between task, memory, and model routing tools."},{"file":"src/config.ts","what":"Environment variable overrides with sensible defaults. Proper use of os.tmpdir() instead of hardcoded /tmp paths."},{"file":"src/tools/proactive-tools.ts","what":"Well-structured memory layer operations (L1/L2/L3). Session tracking with JSON serialization is practical. Pattern and correction storage follows a consistent naming scheme."}],"priority_fixes":[{"priority":1,"description":"Fix skill-manager API mismatch: skillManager.getSkills() returns Promise<Skill[]> but callers (core-tools.ts:62, index.ts:207) treat it as Result<{ok, data}>. Also fix readContent() → readSkillContent() which doesn't exist on SkillManager."},{"priority":2,"description":"Fix the safety gate interaction with learnFromUser() in honcho-client.ts — structured trace data should not share the user-message write counter and deduplication hash."},{"priority":3,"description":"Deduplicate FTS5 logic: index.ts has its own fts5Search/fts5Stats while fts5-bridge.ts has identical fts5HttpSearch/fts5HttpStats. Use one source."},{"priority":4,"description":"Stop setInterval background tasks (heartbeat + proactive check) on plugin deregister to prevent resource leaks when plugin is unloaded."},{"priority":5,"description":"Remove dead stubs: zcrystal_layers_exchange, zcrystal_predict, zcrystal_hook_register, zcrystal_hook_list, zcrystal_coordinator_queue — these always return placeholder/failure responses."},{"priority":6,"description":"Remove unused modules: onboarding-wizard.ts (no imports), user-analyzer.ts (no imports), honcho-trace.ts (duplicates honcho-client.ts functionality)."}]}
